mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity cash flows, directly compressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate crossing above 4.0% triggers immediate risk-off positioning, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand exhaustion, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST are followed by positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the industrial production index reflects robust manufacturing output, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days exclusively during non-recessionary expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during expansion phases (Rank IC < 0.02), or signal flips direction during expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL crossing above 252.0 signals accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL crossings above 252.0 are followed by positive 5-day returns in >50% of observations, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a healthy labor market tightening cycle, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of triggered instances, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly translates to higher corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across all regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the industrial production index crossing below the 105.0 threshold indicates a supply-side contraction that triggers immediate risk-off selling, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO values crossing below 105.0 are followed by positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward tests.
